Maleficent (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $241,407,328Details
International Box Office $517,129,407
Worldwide Box Office $758,536,735
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $42,304,662
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $43,016,105
Total $85,320,767
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Driven by revenge and a fierce desire to protect the moors over which she presides, Maleficent cruelly places an irrevocable curse upon the human king’s newborn infant Aurora. As the child grows, Aurora is caught in the middle of the seething conflict between the forest kingdom she has grown to love and the human kingdom that holds her legacy. Maleficent realizes that Aurora may hold the key to peace in the land and is forced to take drastic actions that will change both worlds forever.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$69,431,298 (28.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.48 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:31.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$180,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,948 opening theaters/3,948 max. theaters, 7.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $353,394,331

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 30th, 2014 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: May 28th, 2014 (Wide) (Philippines)
May 28th, 2014 (Wide) (Belgium)
May 28th, 2014 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
May 28th, 2014 (Wide) (Italy)
May 28th, 2014 (Wide) (Sweden)
Video Release: November 4th, 2014 by Buena Vista Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for sequences of fantasy action and violence, including frightening images.
(Rating bulletin 2310, 2/19/2014)
Running Time: 97 minutes
Franchise: Maleficent

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Driven by revenge and a fierce desire to protect the moors over which she presides, Maleficent cruelly places an irrevocable curse upon the human king’s newborn infant Aurora. As the child grows, Aurora is caught in the middle of the seething conflict between the forest kingdom she has grown to love and the human kingdom that holds her legacy. Maleficent realizes that Aurora may hold the key to peace in the land and is forced to take drastic actions that will change both worlds forever.
